headline: Maternal Nutrition Program Reaches Only 55% of Pregnant B40 Women
context: Government nutrition supplementation program for low-income pregnant women has significant coverage gaps.

cards.big: 45% of pregnant B40 women do not receive any government nutrition support in 2025
cards.sub: Nutritional deficiency during pregnancy causes low birth weight and developmental delays

cards.big: Low birth weight rate among B40 mothers is 14.2% versus 6.8% among T20 mothers
cards.sub: 2-fold gap in birth weight outcomes tracks directly with nutritional support access

cards.big: KKM nutrition supplement allocation of RM180 per pregnant woman covers only 40% of actual needs
cards.sub: Insufficient supplementation budget means smaller portions distributed to fewer recipients

cards.big: Brazil's Bolsa Familia nutrition conditional transfers reduced low birth weight by 34% since 2004
cards.sub: Cash transfers enabling food choice proved more effective than commodity distribution

cards.big: Malnourished mothers produce disadvantaged children—the cycle of poverty begins before birth
Should all B40 pregnant women receive automatic monthly nutrition cash transfers
